coverage.source: reported 100% across collected surface; static read (not --with-run)

checks.evidence: Jest only; no Vitest. Config at jest.config.ts.
RTL present in a React 19 project that ships tests.
user-event installed and used in the component suite.
jest-dom installed.
jest-dom loaded via setupFilesAfterEnv (jest.config.ts:12); jest-axe matcher also registered in the same setup file.
Explicit 80% thresholds across all four metrics; exclusions documented inline.
Project lints with Biome, not ESLint; neither eslint-plugin-testing-library nor eslint-plugin-jest-dom is present. Graph confirms no ESLint nodes in Biome/Dev-Dependencies communities.
checks.gap: Automated selector/async/matcher rules (no-node-access, prefer-screen-queries, prefer-find-by, no-wait-for-multiple-assertions) are unguarded by any linter. The three current tests are clean, so the risk is latent for the next author.
checks.remediation: Adopt Biome equivalents where they exist or add a thin ESLint pass scoped to *.test.{ts,tsx} enabling the two plugins.

checks.evidence: Playwright present; config + behaviour spec + accessibility spec exist.
12/12 (100%) Priority 1: 8 getByRole + 4 getByText. Zero Priority 2/3.
checks.resolution: Four getByTestId('count') calls replaced with getByText/status-region assertions; ratio 66.7% -> 100%.

checks.expectation: At most 10% of all queries use getByTestId.
checks.evidence: 0 getByTestId calls in the entire suite; data-testid removed from ThresholdCounter.tsx (grep across src/ and e2e/ returns nothing). 0% < 10% ceiling.
checks.resolution: Non-accessible data-testid='count' hook removed; count asserted via visible text + role='status' live region.

checks.evidence: Zero container/document.querySelector calls. The one container reference is axe(container) (jest-axe API), not a selector lookup.
All 12 queries are screen.getBy*; render return never destructured for queries. a11y test destructures { container } only to feed axe().
Component render return never captured; a11y test uses { container } (documented field, not a wrapper alias).
8/12 (66.7%) of Priority 1 queries are getByRole with a name; remaining 4 are getByText against visible numeric content (correct Priority 1 for non-interactive text). Above the 50% byRole floor.
getByRole('status') resolves the implicit live-region role of <output> (ThresholdCounter.tsx:25); no redundant [role=...] assertions.
6 userEvent calls, 0 fireEvent -> 100% (threshold 80%).
Synchronous updates; no waitFor(() => getBy*()) misuse; findBy not needed.
No queryBy* usage.
No waitFor in the suite; none of the single-assertion / empty-callback / side-effect misuse patterns can occur.
All 5 act() calls wrap hook state mutations under renderHook (the required RTL pattern), not render/fireEvent. No act(() => render(...)) or act(async ...) anywhere; component test uses none.
No cleanup import or call; Jest auto-cleanup relied upon.
Every query feeds an explicit expect(...); hook test asserts on result.current/return value; a11y test asserts toHaveNoViolations. No bare-query lines.

samples.match: it('warns as it approaches the limit, then reports capacity at the threshold')
it('increments, floors decrement at zero, and resets to the initial value')

checks.evidence: All names describe user-observable or contract behaviour; none assert internal state shape or call sequences.
Zero toHaveClass assertions; E2E specs assert only on status text and roles.
Zero snapshot tests; suite avoids the snapshot smell entirely.

samples.match: documented collectCoverageFrom exclusions (index.ts barrels, main.tsx/App.tsx composition root); feature logic NOT excluded

checks.evidence: 80% thresholds set; exclusions documented inline; collected feature surface (statusForCount, useThresholdCounter, ThresholdCounter) at 100% coverage. Graph Threshold-Counter Feature community confirms all three logic units are on the collected surface and tested.
checks.resolution: Pure rule + hook now directly tested; exclusion rationale documented; App.tsx covered by E2E journey by design.

checks.evidence: coverageThreshold fails jest --coverage on regression; pnpm verify now runs test:coverage (not plain jest); CI test job runs pnpm test:coverage.
checks.resolution: verify gate now coverage-enforcing; CI workflow with a test:coverage job now exists and was observable.

samples.match: walks from healthy through warning to at-capacity, then resets
the landing page has no detectable WCAG A/AA violations

checks.evidence: One full role-driven user journey plus a real-browser WCAG scan. styleproof.spec.ts is a computed-style capture (inert without STYLEMAP_DIR), not behavioural coverage.
Zero fixed waits; per-test render/renderHook + userEvent.setup(); statusForCount cases are an independent it.each table; no shared mutable state; Playwright fullyParallel.
No mocks; component and hook tested as real units, statusForCount tested as a pure function. No async/error paths exist to mock.

samples.match: statusForCount table test — all three branches at boundaries 0/WARNING-1/WARNING/CRITICAL-1/CRITICAL/CRITICAL+1
renderHook test — increment, decrement floored at 0, reset to non-zero initial

checks.evidence: Graph-enriched: the Threshold-Counter Feature community (ThresholdCounter, statusForCount, useThresholdCounter, ThresholdLevel, ThresholdStatus) is fully covered. Pure rule tested in isolation; hook reset-to-non-zero path exercised; a11y asserted at unit (jest-axe) and e2e (axe-core/playwright) layers. App/rootElement are composition-root nodes covered by the E2E journey and excluded by documented config.
checks.resolution: Every prior sub-finding (pure rule untested, no hook-isolation test, no a11y-assertion library) is fixed.
checks.forwardNote: No error-path/async tests yet because the feature is synchronous and cannot throw; the template sets no pattern for that case. Add a findBy* appearance test + error-state assertion when the first async surface lands.
